analysis.summary: Mallika Dasgupta, a 21-year-old female, is experiencing fever, cough, and cold symptoms for 5 days with a severity of 6/10, currently taking Tylenol.

flags.title: Prolonged Symptoms
flags.description: Symptoms lasting 5 days warrant medical evaluation, especially if not improving or worsening.

flags.title: Symptom Severity
flags.description: A self-reported severity of 6/10 indicates moderate discomfort and may require further intervention beyond over-the-counter medication.

possible_conditions.name: Common Cold
possible_conditions.explanation: Symptoms like fever, cough, and cold are highly consistent with a common viral cold.

possible_conditions.name: Influenza (Flu)
possible_conditions.explanation: Influenza can present with similar symptoms, including fever, cough, and body aches, and the 5-day duration is typical for this illness.

possible_conditions.name: Acute Bronchitis
possible_conditions.explanation: If the cough is persistent and possibly productive, bronchitis could be a consideration, often following a viral infection.

possible_conditions.name: Sinusitis (Viral or Bacterial)
possible_conditions.explanation: Prolonged cold symptoms can sometimes develop into sinusitis, especially if there is significant nasal congestion or facial pressure.

recommendations: Consult a healthcare provider for an accurate diagnosis and treatment plan given the duration and severity of symptoms.
Continue symptomatic relief with Tylenol as directed, ensuring not to exceed the recommended daily dosage.
Ensure adequate rest and hydration by drinking plenty of fluids.
Monitor for any worsening symptoms such as difficulty breathing, severe chest pain, high fever unresponsive to medication, or signs of dehydration, and seek immediate medical attention if they occur.
questions_for_doctor: What is the most likely diagnosis for my symptoms?
Are any specific tests (e.g., flu test, strep test) recommended?
What is the best course of treatment for my condition?
Are there any specific warning signs or symptoms I should watch out for that would indicate a more serious condition?
